title: Vanessa Fisk
content:
{{Character |image = Vanessa Marianna Fisk Infobox.png |real name = Vanessa Marianna{{Ref|DD109}} (birth name)
Vanessa Fisk{{Ref|DDBA101}} (married name) |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |gender = Female |title = First Lady of [[New York City]] |affiliation = [[Scene Contempo Gallery]] (formerly)
[[Five Families]] |tv series = ''[[Daredevil (TV series)|Daredevil]]'' (11 episodes)
''[[Daredevil: Born Again]]'' (8 episodes) |actor = [[Ayelet Zurer]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|When I first met you, I was fascinated by your strength. Your brutality so clear to me, even as you hid it from me. I wanted to observe everything you did. And as I did, I fell in love with you.|Vanessa Marianna to [[Wilson Fisk]]|One Last Shot}} First Lady '''Vanessa Fisk''' (''née'' '''Marianna''') is an art dealer, crime lady, wife of [[Wilson Fisk]], and first lady of [[New York City]]. She remained loyal to Fisk even after learning of his criminal empire and vowed to stay with him in the wake of her being [[Attack on Vanessa Marianna|poisoned]] by [[Madame Gao]] and [[Leland Owlsley]], in their attempt to refocus Fisk's attention away from Marianna. When Fisk had been arrested by the [[FBI]], the couple then attempted to escape [[New York City]] together, until [[Daredevil]] interfered as he ensured Fisk was sent to [[Rikers Island]], causing Marianna to go on the run, spending two years overseas. Fisk managed to manipulate the FBI to drop all of their charges on himself and Marianna, allowing her to come home. Marianna married Fisk, but her wedding was crashed by both [[Daredevil]] and [[Benjamin Poindexter]], as Marianna was separated from her husband. They later reunited when he was freed from prison, and she took his surname. When Wilson chose to distance from the life of crime in favor of running for Mayor of New York City, Vanessa took over Wilson's criminal enterprise. She opted to hire Poindexter to kill [[Foggy Nelson]] in order to keep the [[Red Hook Port]] free port loophole in secret, as it was an important source of funds. While dealing with the tensions within the [[Five Families]] and supporting her husband's political endeavors, Vanessa started to feel distant from Wilson. They opted to approach [[Heather Glenn]] for a couple therapy, who helped them unpack their issues, including Vanessa's affair with [[Adam (Lover)|Adam]]. Vanessa's loyalty to Wilson was challenged by [[Luca]], who sought to seize control, and Wilson himself, who revealed that he was keeping Adam prisoner. In both cases, Vanessa chose to side with Fisk and as they resolved their issues, Vanessa joined her husband in ruling over New York. ==Biography== {{MD|Vanessa Fisk (Earth-199999)}} ===Early Life=== ====Trouble with Men==== {{Quote|My father... he was in textiles. He would leave for weeks at a time. He drank. He gambled. His behavior ate at my mother. Crushed her. I never respected her for that.|Vanessa Fisk|Sic Semper Systema}} Vanessa Marianna grew up in a family of a textile worker, who would usually be absent from the house and indulge in drinking and gambling. It had a severe negative effect on her mother's well-being and mental state and Marianna did not respect her for that, deeming her weak.{{Ref|DDBA104}} As an adult, Marianna had bad experience with men but while working at an art exhibition, Marianna met a man who turned out to be a bonafide prince. Marianna appreciated his charm, although knowing that he was just reciting pre-planned lines in order to impress her, and the two had sex.{{Ref|DD105}} Marianna eventually was employed at the [[Scene Contempo Gallery]], becoming an expert with selling their art works to wealthy investors, as she would be able to explain the beauty of works, such as ''[[Rabbit in a Snowstorm]]'' to those who had wished to understand it.{{Ref|DD103}} ===Meeting Wilson Fisk=== ====Selling Rabbit in a Snowstorm==== [[File:Dd103 2571.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna meeting [[Wilson Fisk]] in her [[Scene Contempo Gallery|gallery]]]] {{Quote|People always ask me how we can charge so much for what amounts of just [[Rabbit in a Snowstorm|variations of white]], I tell them it's not about the artist's name or the skill required, not even about the art itself. All that matters is, how does it make you feel?
"It makes me feel alone.|Vanessa Marianna and [[Wilson Fisk]]|Rabbit in a Snowstorm (episode)}} While working at [[Scene Contempo Gallery]], Marianna found herself drawn to [[Wilson Fisk]], who was looking over the paintings that were on display, as Fisk took time to stare closely at an all-white painting named ''[[Rabbit in a Snowstorm]]''. Marianna approached the man and spoke to him about what makes people want to purchase art as well as the appeal of this painting, as she questioned if Fisk was interested or just looking, as he insisted that he was interested. [[File:Vanessa with Wilson (Rabbit in a Snowstorm).jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna and [[Wilson Fisk]] study a [[Rabbit in a Snowstorm|painting]]]] Seeing that Fisk was not speaking much as he continued to stare at the artwork, Marianna noted how she would often be asked how the gallery could charge so much money for a painting that was merely various shades of white, before she insisted that it was not about the artist's name or the skill required, but simply how the artwork made a person feel. When Marianna had then asked Fisk what this particular painting made him feel, he answered that it made him feel alone, as they both looked at the art together.{{Ref|DD103}} Fisk agreed to buy the painting from Marianna, as he had then decided to hang it up in the bedroom in his [[Wilson Fisk's Penthouse|Penthouse]] in order to look at it every morning and evening.{{Ref|DD104}} ====Asked out for Dinner==== [[File:VMariannaMeetsWFiskAgain.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna greets [[Wilson Fisk]] upon his return]] {{Quote|That's it? You're not going to offer to buy every painting here so I can to close up early? Guy actually tried that once."
"A woman that can be bought isn't worth having."
"I'm partial to Italian."
"We agree on more than art.|Vanessa Marianna and [[Wilson Fisk]]|In the Blood}} A few days later, [[Wilson Fisk]] had returned into the [[Scene Contempo Gallery]], where Marianna had greeted him as she questioned how he was enjoying [[Rabbit in a Snowstorm]] after he had purchased the painting from her, to which Fisk explained how it had been hung up in his bedroom in his [[Wilson Fisk's Penthouse|Penthouse]], in order for it to be the last thing he sees before sleep, and the first thing he saw when he awoke, which Marianna had then joked that this was either extremely romantic or extremely sad, while Fisk had claimed that he was hoping that it was the former, which Marianna had smiled at. [[File:WilsonVanessaGalleryEp4-700x390-1-.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna suggests [[Wilson Fisk]] impress her]] Clearly nervous, Fisk then claimed that he wanted to thank Marianna in person for the painting, as she told him that she appreciated the sentiment, and asked if there was anything else that she could help him with. Although he considered backing away, Fisk had then asked Marianna if she would join him for a dinner at the [[Brasserie]], although she claimed that she had to work that evening. When Fisk awkwardly went to leave, Marianna called him back, questioning why he did not consider buying every painting in their gallery so that she could close up early, noting that one man had tried that with her in the past. [[File:VanessaMarianna-AskedOut-S1E4.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna agrees to dinner with [[Wilson Fisk]]]] Having been challenged to continue attempting to impress her, Fisk had then returned into the room and had then told Marianna that any woman who could be bought, was not worth having, which was an answer that had impressed Marianna, as Fisk had clearly considered who she was as a person outside of the large purchases. Wishing to know Fisk better, Marianna then agreed to join him for dinner, noting that she enjoyed Italian food which Fisk had told her that he enjoyed as well, as they shared a laugh together. Marianna then agreed to join Fisk for dinner once she had closed up the gallery for the night.{{Ref|DD104}} ====Dinner with Wilson Fisk==== [[File:Wilson and Vanessa First Date.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna having her dinner with [[Wilson Fisk]]]] {{Quote|The [[New York City|city]]'s really changing."
"Not fast enough."
"I don't know. Be a shame to see all of the character scrubbed away."
"You didn't grow up here, did you?|Vanessa Marianna and [[Wilson Fisk]]|In the Blood}} Marianna met with [[Wilson Fisk]] for their date at the [[Brasserie]], as their waiter poured a glass of wine for Fisk, which he tasted and approved for Marianna to have, as she confirmed that it was delicious. Fisk had confessed to not knowing a lot about the wine, admitting that [[James Wesley]] had recommended it, as Marianna joked that she should be out with Wesley instead, which clearly made Fisk more uncomfortable, while Marianna questioned if Fisk went on many dates, as Fisk then confessed to having become too preoccupied recently. [[File:Vanessa-Marianna-Dinner-Wine.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna and [[Wilson Fisk]] discuss [[New York]]]] As Marianna and Fisk then discussed the restaurant, Fisk explained had just opened in their last week. When Marianna noted that [[New York City]] was really changing, Fisk told her that it was not changing fast enough, although Marianna expressed her desire to not see the character of the city be washed away, resulting in Fisk questioning if she had grown up in the city, which she confirmed that she had not. Marianna then listened as Fisk had confessed to dreaming as a child about living far away from Hell's Kitchen, noting that his [[Marlene Vistain|mother]] had sent him away to live on a farm in the middle of nowhere as a child. [[File:VMariannaDiscussesDesert.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna and [[Wilson Fisk]] discussing {{WPS|Zuppa Inglese|zuppa}}]] Marianna listened as Fisk told her that his time away from New York had allowed him to confirm that the city was in his blood, noting that he would do anything to make it into a better place for people like her. Pleased with this answer, Marianna raised her glass as she and Fisk cheered to the better place. As their evening continued, Fisk had offered to buy Marianna any dessert, which she refused, despite Fisk recommending their {{WPS|Zuppa Inglese}}, which Marianna noted was what children ate a birthday parties, which Fisk then admitted to loving this himself as a child, noting that he may have loved it too much. [[File:VMariannaTakenOutOfRestaurant.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna being taken outside by [[Wilson Fisk]]]] Amused by Fisk's enthusiasm, Marianna agreed to have their Zuppa, as she confessed to always having a weakness for chocolate. While they agreed to try something different, Fisk noted that they could too often get caught up in who they were to risk anything new. When she asked who Fisk was, he told her that he was just a man enjoying the company of the captivating Marianna. However, before their dessert could arrive, [[Anatoly Ranskahov]] burst into the restaurant in an attempt to tell Fisk that he and his [[Vladimir Ranskahov|brother]] had accepted his offer. Fisk then escorted Marianna out from the restaurant as fast as possible.{{Ref|DD104}} ====Saying Goodnight==== [[File:VMariannaWalkedHomeByWFisk.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna being walked home by [[Wilson Fisk]]]] {{Quote|I am not interested in gestures, or your money, or whatever that was all about at the [[Brasserie|restaurant]]. I went out with you because there is something different about you. I'm not so sure it's a good thing now."
"Like you said I don't do this much. And I'm sorry that our night, it went sideways. But I enjoyed our time together very much.|Vanessa Marianna and [[Wilson Fisk]]|In the Blood}} In the wake of [[Anatoly Ranskahov]]'s interruption, Marianna was taken out of [[Brasserie]] and walked to her apartment by [[Wilson Fisk]]. As they arrived, Fisk asked Marianna if he would be able to see her again, to which Marianna claimed that she did not date the customers from [[Scene Contempo Gallery]], while Fisk noted that she came out with him, although she made it clear that she had regrets about that. Fisk had suggested that he could return [[Rabbit in a Snowstorm]] in order to no longer be seen as her customer. [[File:Vanessa-Marianna-After-Date.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna saying goodnight to [[Wilson Fisk]]]] However, Marianna had then told Fisk that she was not interested in gestures, or his money, and she did not care about what Ranskahov had been ranting about at the restaurant. Marianna claimed that she agreed to go out with Fisk since there was something different about him, although she suggested that this was not a good thing. Fisk had then apologized for their night going sideways, insisting that while he enjoyed their night, he would understand if she never wanted to see him again. When Fisk asked how she felt about him, Marianna simply claimed that she did not know how she felt before walking away.{{Ref|DD104}} ====Second Date with Fisk==== [[File:VMariannaArrivesForSecondDate.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna entering the [[Press Lounge Restaurant|restaurant]] for dinner]] {{Quote|I've been lied to before, by men. Some were even decent ones, but they still felt the need to be dishonest about things that mattered. Do you feel that need, Wilson?"
"I don't like to be in public, and I don't like to be questioned. But you can ask me anything, Vanessa. I will always be honest.|Vanessa Marianna and [[Wilson Fisk]]|World on Fire}} In the wake of their disastrous first date, Marianna was then invited out on a second date by [[Wilson Fisk]], this time at the [[Press Lounge Restaurant]], although she considered not attending. Despite her concerns, Marianna decided to join Fisk for the second date, much to his clear delight as [[Marcel]] showed Marianna to Fisk's table. Greeted him, Marianna make it clear that she had considered not turning up, while acknowledging how Fisk rented out the entire Restaurant. [[File:VMariannaDiscussesBeingLiedTo.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna agrees to join [[Wilson Fisk]]'s dinner]] Marianna had questioned what would happen if she decided to leave now, as Fisk reassured her that he would allow her to leave and dine alone, noting that this would not be the first time. Marianna then told Fisk that she had been lied to by other men in the past, as they had felt the need to be dishonest with her, as Marianna questioned if Fisk also needed to be dishonest. Fisk then told Marianna that he did not like being in public or being questioned, but promised that he would always be honest with her. Accepting his answer, Marianna agreed to have her dinner with Fisk, asking what they would be drinking. [[File:Dinner date 2.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna and [[Wilson Fisk]] have their dinner]] As they ate together, Marianna told Fisk about some of her previous relationships, including the prince whom she met at an exhibition she had curated. When Marianna explained how this prince had seduced her and she slept with him, she had then seen that it had made Fisk uncomfortable to hear, although he claimed that he was not shocked, but simply regretted not saying something like that to Marianna when they had first met, only for Marianna to insist that she was glad that he did not. Marianna then commented on the wine, as Fisk explained that [[James Wesley]] had chosen it, noting that he was being honest. [[File:Vanessa-Marianna-Second-Date.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna asking [[Wilson Fisk]] about his work]] Marianna had then listened on while Fisk explained that Wesley was more than just his assistant, but that he was his friend as well, with Marianna teasing her surprise that Fisk had a friend, commenting on how Fisk had claimed to be lonely while he looked at the [[Rabbit in a Snowstorm]]. Marianna questioned why Fisk had felt alone, to which he claimed it was simply the nature of his business, as Marianna then questioned exactly what the nature of his business was, to which he claimed it was rebuilding [[New York City]], as Fisk went on to claim that he wanted to build something beautiful out of the city's ugliness. [[File:VMariannaListeningToWFisk.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna being asked about her hidden gun]] Marianna compared Fisk to an artist, although he claimed to simply be a man with a dream. Changing the subject, Marianna asked Fisk about his cufflinks, noting that Fisk had worn them on their previous date, while Fisk told her that they were his [[Bill Fisk|father]]'s and that he wore them everyday to remember him, explaining that his father [[Murder of Bill Fisk|died]] when he was a child. Fisk then asked Marianna if he could ask her his own question, which she accepted as she had felt that Fisk had earned a question, before Fisk questioned what gun she had in her purse, and asked if she had thought that she might need it during the night. [[File:VMariannaAskingNotToBeInsulted.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna listening to [[Wilson Fisk]]'s belief]] However, Marianna took great offense to this, questioning why Fisk would insult her by acting as though she had no idea about what he really did for a living. Fisk then told her that he had not lied about his intentions for New York, as he claimed that his money and influence would not be enough to really change their city, as he insisted that sometimes change on the scale that he had intended had required force. Marianna then leaned forward and noted that she knew that Fisk was a dangerous man, which was why she had bought the gun, telling him that she would like a reason to stay sat at the table. [[File:WilsonVanessaGunEp5-1-.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna hands her gun over to [[Wilson Fisk]]]] Fisk confessed to Marianna that he had done things that he was not proud of, telling her that he had hurt people and he intended to hurt more, insisting that it would be impossible to avoid for what he was trying to do. Marianna listened closely as Fisk insisted that he took no pleasure in all the pain he caused, but claimed that the city would not be reborn on its own, it first needed to die. Marianna questioned if she needed her gun, to which Fisk told her that when she was by his side, she would be safe, as Marianna had then taken the gun out of her purse and handed it over to Fisk, who put it inside his jacket. [[File:Wilson-Fisk-Vanessa-Marianna-Fire.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Fisk and Marianna overlook the burning city]] As they came to the end of the second date, Marianna and Fisk then witnessed several [[Bombing of Hell's Kitchen|explosions]] being set off across New York, as they went to the window to observe the flames. As Marianna looked on in horror at New York burned around them, Fisk asked if she had read about a [[List of Minor Characters/The Defenders Saga#Cut Man|boy]] who was kidnapped by the [[Russian Mafia]], who beat his [[List of Minor Characters/The Defenders Saga#Into the Ring|father]] into submission before stealing the child away, as he claimed that those men responsible had been killed in all of these explosions. Marianna had considered the implications of this for a brief moment, before making it clear to Fisk that she approved of their deaths. [[File:VMariannaWFiskLeavingSecondDate.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna and Fisk leaving the restaurant]] Having gained her approval of what he did for a living, as well as his methods for improving the city, Fisk then decided to take Marianna home before their [[New York City Police Department]] could shut down all the roads in the aftermath of the explosions, as Fisk gave Marcel a tip as he thanked him for everything that he had done for them that evening, with Marcel being briefly nervous that Fisk was reaching for Marianna's gun that he still had inside of his pocket. Fisk then offered Marianna his hand, which she took before she and Fisk departed the restaurant, ignoring those flames that were engulfing New York.{{Ref|DD105}} ====Falling in Love==== [[File:Vanessa and Wesley.PNG|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna being asked to help [[James Wesley]]]] {{Quote|There are [[Masked Man|people]] out there that want to expose me. They don't understand what I'm trying to do for [[New York City|this city]]. They want to drag my name through the streets. They want to destroy everything that I'm trying to accomplish."
"And are you going to let them?|[[Wilson Fisk]] and Vanessa Marianna|Shadows in the Glass}} Marianna was called up by [[Wilson Fisk]], as they discussed Fisk's time in [[Asia]], only for their conversation to become interrupted as Fisk got another call, as Fisk apologized for having to take a call before wishing Marianna a goodnight. The next evening, Marianna was contacted by [[James Wesley]], who had informed her that Fisk was currently having his emotional breakdown following his unsuccessfully meeting with [[Madame Gao]], as Wesley had asked Marianna to come to Fisk's [[Wilson Fisk's Penthouse|Penthouse]] to calm him down, which Marianna agreed to, despite Fisk insisting he wanted to be alone. [[File:VMariannaAsksToStayWithWFisk.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna attempts to reassure [[Wilson Fisk]]]] Marianna insisted to the clearly concerned Wesley that Fisk would be alright, as Wesley left them alone. As Wesley had then left, Marianna complimented Fisk's Penthouse, jokingly commenting on the table that Fisk had overturned during his rages, only for Fisk to plead with Marianna to leave, while she questioned if this was what Fisk really wanted. Marianna noted that Wesley had become concerned about Fisk, although Fisk had insisted that he did not have to be, to which Marianna reminded Fisk that he had vowed to always be honest with her, as she then questioned what it was that was concerning him. [[File:VMariannaWFisk-StandingByWindow1.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna asking [[Wilson Fisk]] to be honest]] Fisk had then finally confessed that he was afraid, to which Marianna had asked him what he was afraid of, as Fisk claimed to be afraid of how Marianna would look at him if she knew the truth about who he was. Turning around to look Fisk in the eyes, Marianna asked him to tell her the entire truth, as she was told about how Fisk had murdered his own [[Bill Fisk|father]] with his hammer to protect his [[Marlene Vistain|mother]] from his abuse, as the murdered was covered up and blamed on [[Don Rigoletto]], who Fisk's father had owed money to before he died. Marianna listened to Fisk's confession closely, without making a comment throughout. [[File:VanessaMarianna-WilsonFisk-Comfort.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna listening to [[Wilson Fisk]]'s past life]] Marianna attempted to assure Fisk that this situation was not his own fault, as he had protected his mother, although Fisk claimed that he did not kill his father for his mother, but for himself, noting that he still wore his father's [[Wilson Fisk's Cufflinks|cufflinks]] to remind him not to be cruel for the sake of cruelty. Fisk then asked Marianna if she believed he was a monster like his father was, as she reassured him that he was not. However, Fisk claimed that this no longer mattered, as there were people out there who wanted to expose his crimes against [[New York City]], while claiming that they did not understand what he was trying to do. [[File:VMariannaWFisk-StandingByWindow2.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna agrees to still stand by [[Wilson Fisk]]]] As Fisk claimed that there were people who wanted to drag his name through the dirt and destroy everything that he had been attempting to accomplish on behalf of rebuilding New York into something new and beautiful, Marianna had then simply questioned if he was going to let them do all this to them, or if he was willing to continue to fight for what he believed was best for New York. Shocked that Marianna was not more horrified by his confessions, Fisk told her that he would not allow them to destroy him or all his plans, as Marianna held onto Fisk's arm while the pair of them had looked out over New York.{{Ref|DD108}} ===Remaining Loyal=== ====Standing by Wilson Fisk==== [[File:WFiskVMarianna-FirstBreakfast.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna and [[Wilson Fisk]] having breakfast]] {{Quote|The last thing I wanted was for anyone close to me to become a target from those who do not share my dream. For those who will have [[New York City|this city]] stay exactly as it is, mired in poverty and crime. But I know now it was foolish to make that decision. That I can no longer do it alone.|[[Wilson Fisk]]|Shadows in the Glass}} Following all of [[Wilson Fisk]]'s confessions over his past life, Marianna had decided to spend that night in his [[Wilson Fisk's Penthouse|penthouse]]. During their night, Marianna had slept beside Fisk, as he awoke due to a bad dream and looked at the [[Rabbit in a Snowstorm]], only for him to turn back over to Marianna and embrace her, which helped Fisk sleep. In the morning, Marianna had a shower before she rejoined Fisk at his dining room table and they had enjoyed eating some breakfast together. [[File:Vanessa-Marianna-Wilson-Fisk-Dressing.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna helps picking [[Wilson Fisk]]'s outfit]] Marianna managed to convince Fisk to take some more chances with his life, as she joined him in choosing a new suit for him to wear out in [[New York City]], with Marianna selecting a grey suit with a black shirt, rather than the all black suits that he would normally wear. Marianna took great pleasure in helping Fisk step out of his comfort zone, even forcing him not to wear his [[Bill Fisk|father]]'s [[Wilson Fisk's Cufflinks|cufflinks]], but ones that Marianna picked out for him. Once Fisk was dressed, Marianna completed his look by placing a handkerchief in his suit pocket, but allowing Fisk to admire himself in the mirror, satisfied with Marianna's selections. [[File:WHiH - Wilson Fisk Pledges Aid to Hell's Kitchen.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna stands by [[Wilson Fisk]] on the news]] Marianna had then stood by Fisk, as they went outside the [[New York State Supreme Court Building]] and gave a speech in front of all of the New York media, including [[WHiH World News]]. Marianna had looked at Fisk with pride, as he told the people of New York that they must not bow down in fear to the actions of the [[Daredevil|masked man]], as Fisk had gained huge approval. With Fisk telling the world that he was a man seeking to help the city and could no longer do it without support, he introduced himself.{{Ref|DD108}} The following day, the [[New York Bulletin]] wrote their praising article about Fisk, where they mentioned Marianna.{{Ref|DD109}} ====Meeting Matt Murdock==== [[File:Vanessa Marianna meets Matt Murdock.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna warmly greeting [[Matt Murdock]]]] {{Quote|I'm told by my guests my [[Matt Murdock's Apartment|apartment]]'s a bit stark. I thought maybe some art would warm it up."
"I'm going to go out on a limb and assume these guests were women?"
"Well, I'm not trying to impress the pizza guy."
"Good. You don't need sight to appreciate art, but you do need honesty.|[[Matt Murdock]] and Vanessa Marianna|Speak of the Devil}} While she was working inside the [[Scene Contempo Gallery]], Marianna had greeted [[Matt Murdock]] once he arrived into their gallery, with Marianna then introducing herself to Murdock, and offering her help. Murdock acknowledged the strange nature of a blind man coming into an art gallery, which Marianna laughed at before Murdock explained that some of his guests told him that his [[Matt Murdock's Apartment|apartment]] could use some new artworks to brighten it up, as Marianna jokingly questioned if these guests Murdock had mentioned were actually women. [[File:Matt Murdock and Vanessa Marianna.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna and [[Matt Murdock]] discuss the art]] Marianna told Murdock that he did not need sight to appreciate art, only honesty, although Murdock jokingly claimed that sight did help. Marianna went on to claim that there was something deeply intimate about experiencing art from somebody else's eyes, while noting that it would make a good line and Murdock should use it with any future women. As Marianna led him through the gallery, Murdock claimed to not have a clear idea of what he wanted, Marianna claimed that this was good, noting that art was not furniture, and should speak to him and move him, before she took Murdock over towards one piece. [[File:Matt Murdock & Vanessa Marianna.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna describes the art to [[Matt Murdock]]]] As Marianna took Murdock to one of her favorite pieces, he asked her to describe it to him, as she told him about the sea of tonal reds that represented anger as well as the heart, commenting on how the piece struck the perfect balance between the two emotions. When Murdock claimed that it sounded aggressive, Marianna insisted that it all depended on his point of view, as Murdock suggested that they find something a little less challenging. Murdock asked Marianna if she had a man in her life, to which she jokingly questioned if he was flirting with her, although Murdock insisted that a man who had won her heart clearly would be able to provide a good opinion on art. [[File:Fisk meets Matt Murdock.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna introduces Murdock to [[Wilson Fisk]]]] Before she answered, Marianna noted that Murdock could simply ask him himself, noticing that [[Wilson Fisk]] had just arrived into the gallery. As Marianna then greeted Fisk, he expressed a hope that he was not interrupting, as Marianna noted that they were just talking about him. Marianna introduced Fisk to Murdock, as Fisk recognized Murdock's name through his law firm, [[Nelson and Murdock]], which Murdock had run along with [[Foggy Nelson]]. Marianna explained that Murdock was looking to purchase some artwork from her, as she had noted that he wanted to know Fisk's opinions on what he should buy, to which Fisk simply advised Murdock to buy anything that Marianna selected. [[File:VMariannaTeasingWFiskInGallery.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna teases [[Wilson Fisk]] for his words]] Marianna listened closely as Murdock noted that he and Fisk were on opposite sides of a legal case involving [[Elena Cardenas]]' building, as Fisk was trying to have destroyed after he had bought it from [[Armand Tully]]. As Fisk spoke about his views on how [[New York City]] had been suffering for long enough and could be reborn, Marianna interrupted him by noting that Murdock was her customer, and not one of Fisk's donors, which Fisk had accepted. Apologizing for the hard sell about his viewpoints on their future, Fisk had then insisted to Murdock that New York City's future had remained extremely important to him. [[File:VMariannaWatchesMMurdockLeave.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna watches as [[Matt Murdock]] leaves]] As Marianna stood by, Murdock simply told Fisk that he felt the same way about New York, before he turned to Marianna and thanked her for her time showing him around the gallery. Marianna questioned if Murdock had just changed his mind about what he was looking for, although Murdock insisted that he simply had to reconsider the cost of the artworks that he had been looking at. With that, Murdock then addressed Fisk before walking out of the gallery, which had left both Marianna and Fisk somewhat confused about what Murdock's actual intentions had been with coming into the gallery to speak to her.{{Ref|DD109}} ====[[Attack on Vanessa Marianna|Assassination Attempt]]==== [[File:WFiskVMarianna-BlackSuitSpeech.png|{{AltFloat}}|Marianna sees [[Wilson Fisk]] give his speech]] {{Quote|That was beautiful."
"That word is meant only for you tonight.|Vanessa Marianna and [[Wilson Fisk]]|The Ones We Leave Behind}} Marianna was taken to a benefit that [[Wilson Fisk]] was hosting to gain funding for his organization. There she stood beside Fisk as he gave a speech to those attendees at the [[Van Lunt Building]] about his plans for [[Hell's Kitchen]], promising that all the residents of New York would eventually see a brighter day. Once Fisk had finished his speech, he kissed Marianna, who called that speech beautiful, only for Fisk to insist that the word had only belonged to her, before they made their way through the guests wishing to speak to Fisk, as he thanked them for the donations. For both sides.|Vanessa Fisk to [[Heather Glenn]]|Optics}} Marianna changed her surname to Fisk and helped her [[Wilson Fisk|husband]] run his criminal empire when he returned to the underworld,{{Ref|DDBA101}} during which Wilson told Vanessa of Matt Murdock's identity as [[Daredevil]].{{Ref|DDBA108}} In [[2024|December 2024]], Wilson was shot in the face by [[Maya Lopez]]{{Ref|HE106}} and spent months recovering in the hospital.{{Ref|EC101}} Vanessa stayed by his bedside during his long recovery while she also single-handedly ran his criminal empire in his absence.{{Ref|DDBA101}} In [[2025]],{{Ref|EC101}} Vanessa was surprised that Wilson had recovered but had left the hospital without a word{{Ref|DDBA101}} to travel to [[Tamaha]], [[Oklahoma]].{{Ref|EC104}} Vanessa struggled with Wilson's absence but continued to run his empire, which she enjoyed doing. Using a mixture of money and fear, Vanessa kept organized crime groups, such as the [[Five Families]], in line while simultaneously protecting Kingpin's criminal assets from scrutiny and investigation by legitimizing the streams of revenue and laundering other illicit funds. When Wilson returned to [[New York City]] sometime later, Vanessa was upset that he did not return to her side, and that instead of retaking control of his empire, he had isolated himself and had chosen to become the city's mayor instead. Vanessa focused her efforts on running Kingpin's empire, and due to Wilson's absence, allowed herself to engage in a sexual relationship with [[Adam (Lover)|Adam]],{{Ref|DDBA101}} an artist whose talent she greatly admired.{{Ref|DDBA104}} ====Recruiting Benjamin Poindexter==== [[File:Vanessa Fisk and Benjamin Poindexter.png|{{AltFloat}}|Fisk visits [[Benjamin Poindexter]]]] {{Quote|Benjamin, I'm offering you a chance to get your mind back. Your self... This is no way to live. You are the very best at this type of thing. That's why I came. Your history with my [[Kingpin|husband]] isn't part of this.|Vanessa Fisk to [[Benjamin Poindexter]]|Straight to Hell}} In [[2025]], Vanessa found out that [[Foggy Nelson]] of [[Nelson, Murdock & Page]] was working on [[Benny Cafaro]]'s case and discovered [[Red Hook Port]]'s status. She knew that if Nelson was to use it in court to clear Cafaro of charges, it would shut down the loophole Vanessa had been using and cut off her revenue stream. In order to silence both of them, Vanessa decided to hire [[Benjamin Poindexter]] despite his attempt on her life some years ago, so she pulled some strings with the [[United States Department of Justice]] to get him released. [[File:Vanessa asks Dex to kill Foggy.png|{{AltFloat}}|Vanessa gives [[Benjamin Poindexter]] his targets]] Vanessa visited Poindexter at the mental institution he was held at and talked about [[Ray Nadeem]] and the whole [[FBI]] corruption scandal, mentioning that it allowed [[Kingpin]] to get acquitted. Vanessa said that Poindexter deserved the same and handed him the appeal documents that he needed to sign to get released. Poindexter asked whether Wilson knew about that, to which Vanessa said that her husband had been out of business for a while. As Poindexter guessed correctly that Vanessa wanted him to kill someone, she told him about Cafaro and Nelson, the latter of whom Poindexter was familiar with. [[File:Vanessa Fisk (2025).png|{{AltFloat}}|Vanessa asks [[Benjamin Poindexter]] to trust her]] Vanessa told him that the case they were involved in was threatening to expose her business and clarified that the hit needed to be done soon, within next few days. When the nurse passed by Poindexter and handed him his medication, Vanessa took the pills away and took his hand, saying that she wanted to give Poindexter a chance to get his life back. She praised Poindexter's exceptional skills and assured him that his history with Wilson was not a part of this, which Poindexter disagreed with. Vanessa was still able to get Poindexter to trust him and he signed the paperwork,{{Ref|DDBA109}} successfully carrying out the attack on Cafaro and Nelson soon after, so the secret of Red Hook Port was safe.{{Ref|DDBA101}} ====Return of Wilson Fisk==== [[File:Vanessa Fisk speaks to Five Families.png|{{AltFloat}}|Vanessa manages the [[Five Families]]]] {{Quote|You left the bed I sat beside for months while you recovered. And for what? And now you walk in here and end a meeting with the [[Five Families]] by simply standing in the doorway.|Vanessa Fisk to [[Wilson Fisk]]|Heaven's Half Hour}} In [[2026]], Vanessa presided over a meeting with the [[Five Families]], and listened as [[Viktor (Five Families)|Viktor]] and [[Luca]] squabbled over taxes that were to be paid in the [[Red Hook Port]] territory. Putting her hands on Luca's shoulders, Vanessa reminded them that they should not argue about meager sums of money when they were making millions and that Red Hook was exempt for any taxes, tolls, or tariffs. As she took her place at the head of the table, Vanessa informed the Five Families that they had earned $7.73 million that month, which would be divided and paid to each of them via wire transfer, and asked that they also use wire transfer to send her percentage of their monthly profits. As she added that she could make arrangements if they were paying in cash, they were interrupted by the sound of the warehouse door opening. [[File:Vanessa Fisk speaks to her husband.png|{{AltFloat}}|Vanessa remarks on [[Wilson Fisk]]'s return]] Vanessa turned around and saw her husband [[Wilson Fisk]] standing there, who told her that he needed to speak with her. As the members of the Five Families swiftly left, Vanessa chided Wilson for leaving her upon his recovery and then having stayed away further only to come back and prematurely cancel an important meeting with the Families. Wilson commended Vanessa for keeping the criminals in line and added that they respected her, to which Vanessa retorted that they only respected the money she made for them, and that profit was what made them loyal. [[File:Vanessa Fisk (2026).png|{{AltFloat}}|Vanessa discusses their next steps with [[Wilson Fisk]]]] She then asked her husband how long he was returning for, and Wilson responded that he would be there as long as Vanessa wanted him. Wilson stated that during his recovery process, his love for Vanessa and his desire to help [[New York City]] had been reignited. He notified Vanessa that during his mayoral campaign, his assets could not be perceived as illegal, and Vanessa assured him that she had structured their businesses to elude scrutiny. Wilson then told her that he had the sufficient signatures required to run for mayor and that it was time for him to officially declare his candidacy.{{Ref|DDBA101}} ====Mayor Fisk's Victory==== [[File:Vanessa at Fisk's Mayoral Victory.png|{{AltFloat}}|Vanessa celebrates [[Wilson Fisk]] becoming the Mayor]] {{Quote|I know about [[Adam (Lover)|Adam]], Vanessa."
"Do not kill him."
"I'm not that man anymore.|[[Wilson Fisk]] and Vanessa Fisk|Heaven's Half Hour}} Using the funds from her criminal enterprise, Vanessa bankrolled [[Wilson Fisk]]'s political campaign, which allowed him to compete despite him joining the race late. Wilson's platform aimed at fixing [[New York City]]'s problems and outlawing vigilantism was received well by the citizens and steadily gathered support. In the end, Wilson won the race and was elected Mayor, which meant Vanessa was now the First Lady of New York. She celebrated Wilson's victory with him and the rest of his campaign team, but chose to keep her distance. [[File:Vanessa cheated on Wilson Fisk.png|{{AltFloat}}|Vanessa talks to [[Wilson Fisk]] about her affair]] Later, the Fisks met at the rooftop to talk in private after Wilson made a public statement in regards of his victory. Wilson revealed that he was affair about Vanessa's affair with [[Adam (Lover)|Adam]], which deeply troubled her and she asked Wilson not to kill him. Although he promised that he was not that kind of person anymore, Vanessa was painfully aware of how cruel her husband could be and made him promise her that he would not touch Adam. As Wilson gave his promise, Vanessa walked out of the rooftop and left her husband to overlook the city.{{Ref|DDBA101}} ====Marriage Crisis==== [[File:Mr. and Mrs. Fisk.png|{{AltFloat}}|The Fisks at the couple therapy with [[Heather Glenn]]]] {{Quote|I was lonely. I missed [[Wilson Fisk|Wilson]]. Then he returns and I really don't know how I feel about that.|Vanessa Fisk to [[Heather Glenn]]|Optics}} Due to [[Wilson Fisk]]'s new position as the Mayor as well as her affair with [[Adam (Lover)|Adam]] being out in the open, Vanessa started to grow distant from her husband. While supporting his political endeavors, Vanessa chose not to participate in his public narrative and focused on running their criminal empire. Concerned about their marriage, the Fisks decided to seek couple therapy and approached [[Heather Glenn]] as their therapist. Accompanied by [[Buck Cashman]], Vanessa and her husband entered her [[Heather Glenn's Office|office]] and Glenn asked them to voice their reason for seeking therapy. [[File:Vanessa Fisk (2027).png|{{AltFloat}}|Vanessa talks about her feelings about [[Wilson Fisk]]]] Given the word by Glenn, Vanessa talked about how she and Wilson went through a lot in the past few years and ran a very complicated business together, not mentioning its illegal nature. She then spoke out about Wilson disappearing on her without a notice, prompting Wilson to clarify that he was assaulted by [[Maya Lopez]] and could not stay in touch with her throughout his recovery. Glenn asked Vanessa about her feelings on Wilson's absence, so Vanessa said that it could have been for the best in some ways, as she took over their business and how it thrived under her leadership. But then Glenn asked if it was fulfilling for her personally, Vanessa admitted that she was lonely and missed her husband, unsure on how to feel about his sudden return. Answering Glenn's initial question on why did they decide to seek therapy, Vanessa said that she felt like they had no choice in the matter. She then listened to Wilson talking about his reasons, as he simply remarked that he did not want to lose his wife.{{Ref|DDBA102}} ====Breakfast at Gracie Mansion==== {{Quote|[[Five Families|They]]'re misbehaving because there's no one at the head of the table. "They're rats. Rats at the bottom of the barrel. Let them kill each other."
"And when they're done with each other, whoever is left standing will come after you.|Vanessa Fisk and [[Wilson Fisk]]|The Hollow of His Hand}} ''To be added'' ====Second Counseling Session==== {{Quote|Is that what it is? Chaos before a stronger peace? Or is it my penance? For what I did."

"That was me. You know the rules. Play nice. Keep smart. Keep low. You did none of that. Now you owe.|Vanessa Fisk and [[Luca]]|Art for Art's Sake}} ''To be added'' ====Learning the Truth==== {{Quote|Why would you keep all this from me?"
